title: 3rd International Conference on Biotechnology and Bioinformatics (ICBAB-2026)
content: 3rd International Conference on Biotechnology and Bioinformatics (ICBAB-2026) Frontiers in Sustainable Healthcare, Agriculture, Environment and Computational Biology May 28-30, 2026 Jaypee University of Information Technology, Waknaghat, Solan, H.P. Register Now Tracks Workshops Event Starts In: Days Hours Mins Secs Brochure Registration Guidelines Abstract Dates Opens Jan 15 Closes May 6 Acceptance May 10 Registration Dates Early Bird Mar 30 Normal May 10 Late May 11 About the Conference The 3rd International Conference of Biotechnology and Bioinformatics (ICBAB-2026) aims to bring together leading academicians, industry experts, research scientists, and scholars to exchange and share their knowledge, experiences, and findings across diverse domains of biotechnology and bioinformatics. The goal of the conference is to provide a dynamic platform for updating and disseminating advancements in sustainable healthcare, agriculture, the environment, and computational biology. The event includes interactive workshops, keynote lectures, and discussion forums to foster collaboration. 3 Days 4 Tracks 20+ Speakers 200+ Attendees About JUIT The Jaypee University of Information Technology (JUIT) was established in 2002 following a joint initiative of the Government of Himachal Pradesh and the Founder Chairman of the Jaypee Group, Shri Jai Prakash Gaur. The State Government provided land on lease, and the university was constituted under Act No. 14 of 2002, as notified in the Extraordinary Gazette dated May 23, 2002. JUIT received approval from the University Grants Commission under Section 2(f) of the UGC Act in December 2002 and is a member of the Association of Indian Universities (AIU). Academic activities at JUIT began in July 2002. JUIT currently offers a diverse range of undergraduate, postgraduate, and doctoral programs. B.Tech. programs are available in disciplines including Bioinformatics, Biotechnology, Civil Engineering, Computer Science and Engineering, Electronics and Communication Engineering, Electronics and Computer Engineering, and Information Technology. The university also offers M.Tech. programs with various specializations, along with BBA, B.Sc. Mathematics, and M.Sc. programs in Physics, Biotechnology, and Microbiology. Ph.D. programs are offered across multiple fields such as Biotechnology, Bioinformatics, Engineering, Humanities and Social Sciences, Mathematics, Physics, and Material Science. About the Department Department of Biotechnology & Bioinformatics The Department of Biotechnology and Bioinformatics at JUIT is a hub of cutting-edge research and interdisciplinary learning. We bridge the gap between biological sciences and computational technologies to address critical challenges in healthcare, agriculture, and the environment. Modern Laboratory Infrastructure IPR & Patents Research & Development Program Pre-conference Workshops Intensive hands-on sessions designed for students, researchers, and professionals. Workshop I Industrial Plant Tissue Culture From Lab to Market: Scaling Green Technologies Coordinator: Professor (Dr.) Hemant Sood An intensive one-day hands-on workshop designed to bridge the gap between theory, laboratory practice, and commercial production. Participants will gain practical exposure to in-vitro techniques and micropropagation protocols for Elite Plant species along with transplantation to Hydroponic systems. Key Highlights: Fundamentals and industrial scope of Plant Tissue Culture Hands-on demonstration of micropropagation techniques Media preparation and establishment of Cultures Scale-up strategies and commercial production models like Hydroponics Register for Workshop I Workshop II Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) Data Analysis From Raw Reads to Biological Insights: Decoding Genomic Data Coordinator: Dr.Shikha Mittal An intensive one-day hands-on workshop highlighting how NGS-driven data interpretation powers modern research. Participants gain exposure to NGS data formats, quality control, read alignment, variant detection, and functional annotation. Key Highlights: Overview of NGS technologies and experimental workflows Understanding NGS data formats and quality assessment Hands-on training in read pre-processing and alignment Introduction to variant calling and data interpretation Register for Workshop II Chief Guest Prof. P. L. Gautam Eminent Agricultural Scientist & Academic Administrator Prof. P. L. Gautam is a highly distinguished figure in Indian agricultural research and education, having made monumental contributions to agro-biodiversity management and structural policymaking. Over his illustrious career, he has served in premier leadership capacities, including as the Chairperson of the National Biodiversity Authority (NBA), Vice-Chancellor of G.B. Pant University of Agriculture and Technology, and Vice-Chancellor of Dr. Y.S. Parmar University of Horticulture and Forestry, Solan. His extensive expertise spans plant breeding, genetic resource conservation, and institutional development, bridging critical segments of research with sustainable execution. We are deeply privileged to welcome him as our Chief Guest to grace the grand opening of ICBAB-2026, where his vision and insights will help inspire scientific collaborations across biotechnology and bioinformatics sectors. Eminent Speakers Dr. Yosi Shacham-Diamond Professor, Reichman University, Israel Renowned for his work in silicon-based nano-technologies, specifically electroless plating for ULSI interconnects, flexible electronics, and the development of advanced electrochemical biosensors. Dr. R. K. Sani Professor, South Dakota Mines, South Dakota, USA Conducts fundamental and applied research in bioenergy, leading the NSF-funded BuG ReMeDEE consortium while serving in key editorial and leadership roles within the industrial microbiology community. Dr. Yen Wah Tong Professor, National University of Singapore, Singapore Expertise lies in polymeric biomaterials, particularly their synthesis and surface functionalization, applied to tissue engineering and molecular imprinting, alongside research in algal bioenergy and biologics for sustainable and biomedical innovations. Prof. Xue-Jun Li Professor, University of Illinois, Chicago, USA Dr. Li is a neuroscientist specializing in human pluripotent stem cell–based models to study motor neuron development, degeneration, and regeneration. Her research integrates 3D neural tissue models and patient-specific neurons to uncover disease mechanisms and identify therapeutic targets for motor neuron disorders and spinal cord injury. Dr. Sudesh Kumar Yadav Director, CSIR-IHBT, Palampur Focuses on metabolic engineering and synthetic biology to optimize plant traits and microbial processes, leveraging nanobiology and omics to derive high-value functional products from bioresources. Prof. B. Kundu Professsor, Indian Institute of Technology, New Delhi Specializes in protein engineering and biophysics to decipher the mechanisms of neurodegenerative diseases like ALS and identify therapeutic targets against multi-drug resistant pathogens. Dr. Indrani Ghosh Innovation Protection Unit (IPU), CSIR, New Delhi Specializes in Intellectual Property management and Technology Transfer, having handled extensive global patent portfolios and commercialization efforts while providing IP consultancy to the scientific community. Dr. Anshu Bhardwaj Scientist F, CSIR - IMTECH, Chandigarh Dr. Anshu Bhardwaj is a computational biologist whose research focuses on genome variation analysis, SNP prioritization, and genotype–phenotype correlations, with significant contributions to drug discovery and systems biology. She is also a recognized science communicator and innovator, having led pioneering crowdsourcing initiatives and served on international editorial and advisory platforms. Dr. Santosh K. Gupta ICAR - Indian Institute of Millets Research, Hyderabad As a highly accomplished professional Plant Biologist with extensive experience in research management, research in plant science, teaching, and professional service. I am confident in my ability as a translational scientist for crop sciences and as an editor. With a proven track record of successful collaborations, effective communications and publications, I am well-positioned to contribute to research and development in any organization. Dr. Meena Krishania Choudhary Scientist C, CIAB, Mohali Dr. Meena Krishania Choudhary is a leading expert in biochemical engineering, specializing in biomass valorization, bioprocessing, and industrial biotechnology. With over a decade of impactful research, her work focuses on developing sustainable biomanufacturing processes for high-value biomolecules, functional foods, and carbon capture solutions. She has received several prestigious national and international recognitions for her contributions to science and innovation. Publication Partners Promotional Partners
